topic: summer heat — shop has no AC, considering buying one

turns.content: I know it is only February but... I am already thinking about summer. last year in July the shop was like a hamam. no air conditioning... just a fan that moves hot air around. I cannot do another summer like that
Istanbul summers are brutal, and if you're standing all day working close to customers in a small space, that heat is serious. Are you thinking about getting an actual AC unit installed?
yes... but I do not know anything about these machines. I know razors, I know scissors... air conditioning is not my world. the shop is maybe 35, 40 square meters. what should I look for?

For a 35-40 square meter shop, you'd want a split system AC — an indoor unit on the wall and a compressor outside. Something in the 18,000 BTU range would be right, especially since the door is opening and closing all day with customers. Brands like Arcelik, Vestel, Samsung, or Daikin are commonly available in Turkey. Arcelik and Vestel are solid Turkish brands with good local service. For budget, you're looking at roughly 15,000-25,000 lira for the unit plus 3,000-5,000 for installation.

20,000 to 30,000 total... oof. everything costs money. but last August I lost customers because of the heat. one man told me, 'Mehmet, I love you but I am not sitting in this oven for 40 minutes.' he went to the new place on the corner... they have air conditioning, of course

That's the calculation right there. If you're losing even 2-3 customers a day in July and August, that's probably 15,000-20,000 lira in lost revenue over the summer. The unit pays for itself in one season. Plus your own comfort matters — standing in that heat all day takes a real physical toll.

you are right... my back is bad enough without sweating through my shirt for 10 hours. and I need the landlord's permission to mount the compressor on the outside wall. after the rent negotiation I do not want to ask him for anything... but the tailor next door has one, so he should say yes. maybe I bring him some tea first. in Turkey we do not ask for favors without tea

Tea first, then the favor — that's good diplomacy. You can also remind him the AC adds value to his property even after your lease ends. It should be an easy yes. Try to get it sorted by March or April so you're not scrambling when the heat comes.

yes... spring, I buy it. get it installed before June. I will look at the Arçelik ones, good Turkish brand. Deniz already complains about the heat and he is 17... wait until he is my age. anyway, thank you for the advice my friend. I feel better having a plan. güle güle
You've got a solid plan. Arcelik, 18,000 BTU, installed by spring — you'll wonder how you survived without it. Güle güle, Mehmet. Stay warm until then.
